name: bamboohr
description: Look up BambooHR employees, check who's out, and read or create time-off requests

envVars.name: BAMBOOHR_API_KEY
envVars.description: BambooHR API key, used as the HTTP Basic username. Generate it from your name menu in BambooHR under API Keys.

envVars.name: BAMBOOHR_API_PASSWORD
envVars.description: HTTP Basic password. BambooHR ignores the value - any string works; the conventional value is x.

envVars.name: BAMBOOHR_SUBDOMAIN
envVars.description: Your BambooHR company subdomain - the {company} part of https://{company}.bamboohr.com. Pass it as the companyDomain parameter on each tool.

tools.name: Get Employee Directory
tools.description: Get the company employee directory with names, job titles, departments, and contact details

companyDomain.description: BambooHR company subdomain (the {company} in {company}.bamboohr.com), also stored as BAMBOOHR_SUBDOMAIN

Accept.description: Response format; keep application/json (BambooHR returns XML otherwise)

tools.name: Get Employee
tools.description: Get a single employee record with the specific fields you request

companyDomain.description: BambooHR company subdomain (the {company} in {company}.bamboohr.com), also stored as BAMBOOHR_SUBDOMAIN

employeeId.description: Employee ID; use 0 for the employee tied to the API key

fields.description: Comma-separated field names to return, e.g. firstName,lastName,jobTitle,department,workEmail,hireDate,supervisor

onlyCurrent.description: Set to false to include future-dated values from history table fields

Accept.description: Response format; keep application/json (BambooHR returns XML otherwise)

tools.name: List Time-Off Requests
tools.description: List time-off requests that overlap a date range, optionally filtered by employee or status

companyDomain.description: BambooHR company subdomain (the {company} in {company}.bamboohr.com), also stored as BAMBOOHR_SUBDOMAIN

start.description: Start of the date range, YYYY-MM-DD. Requests overlapping the range are returned.

end.description: End of the date range, YYYY-MM-DD

employeeId.description: Only return requests for this employee ID

status.description: Comma-separated statuses: approved, denied, superseded, requested, canceled

type.description: Comma-separated time-off type IDs to include

action.description: Limit to requests the API user can act on: view or approve

Accept.description: Response format; keep application/json (BambooHR returns XML otherwise)

tools.name: Create Time-Off Request
tools.description: Create a time-off request for an employee; it must be approved before it appears in history

companyDomain.description: BambooHR company subdomain (the {company} in {company}.bamboohr.com), also stored as BAMBOOHR_SUBDOMAIN

employeeId.description: Employee ID the request is for

Accept.description: Response format; keep application/json (BambooHR returns XML otherwise)

timeOffTypeId.description: Time-off type ID (e.g. vacation or sick); list types via the company's time-off types in BambooHR

start.description: First day of time off, YYYY-MM-DD

end.description: Last day of time off, YYYY-MM-DD

status.description: Request status: requested, approved, or denied. Approving directly requires sufficient permissions.

amount.description: Total amount of time off in the unit of the time-off type (days or hours). Provide amount and/or per-day dates.

tools.name: List Time-Off Types
tools.description: List the company's time-off types and their IDs - needed to fill the timeOffTypeId field when creating a time-off request

companyDomain.description: BambooHR company subdomain (the {company} in {company}.bamboohr.com), also stored as BAMBOOHR_SUBDOMAIN

Accept.description: Response format; keep application/json (BambooHR returns XML otherwise)

tools.name: Update Time-Off Request Status
tools.description: Approve, deny, or cancel an existing time-off request (the API user must have approval permissions)

companyDomain.description: BambooHR company subdomain (the {company} in {company}.bamboohr.com), also stored as BAMBOOHR_SUBDOMAIN

requestId.description: Time-off request ID (from List Time-Off Requests)

Accept.description: Response format; keep application/json (BambooHR returns XML otherwise)

status.description: New status for the request: approved, denied, or canceled

note.description: Optional note to attach to the status change

tools.name: List Field Metadata
tools.description: List all employee fields available in the account (standard and custom) so agents know which field names Get Employee accepts

companyDomain.description: BambooHR company subdomain (the {company} in {company}.bamboohr.com), also stored as BAMBOOHR_SUBDOMAIN

Accept.description: Response format; keep application/json (BambooHR returns XML otherwise)

tools.name: List Who's Out
tools.description: List approved time off and company holidays in a date range to see who is out of office

companyDomain.description: BambooHR company subdomain (the {company} in {company}.bamboohr.com), also stored as BAMBOOHR_SUBDOMAIN

start.description: Start of the date range, YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to today

end.description: End of the date range, YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to 14 days from the start date

Accept.description: Response format; keep application/json (BambooHR returns XML otherwise)

prompts.title: Who's out this week
prompts.prompt: Check BambooHR for who is out of office this week and summarize by team.

prompts.title: Look up an employee
prompts.prompt: Find an employee I name in the BambooHR directory and show their job title, department, and contact details.

prompts.title: Pending time-off requests
prompts.prompt: List BambooHR time-off requests with status requested for the next month and summarize what needs approval.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: BambooHR setup

steps.title: Get a BambooHR account
steps.description: Use your company's existing BambooHR account, or start a free trial at https://www.bamboohr.com to get a test company. Note your subdomain - the {company} part of https://{company}.bamboohr.com.

steps.title: Generate an API key
steps.description: Log in to BambooHR, click your name in the lower-left corner, and choose API Keys to generate a key. The key inherits the permissions of your user, so time-off and directory access depend on what your user can see.

steps.title: Store the credentials
steps.description: Add BAMBOOHR_API_KEY=<your key> and BAMBOOHR_SUBDOMAIN=<your subdomain> to your .env file. The API uses HTTP Basic auth with the API key as the username and any string as the password (BAMBOOHR_API_PASSWORD defaults to x).

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the Get Employee Directory tool with companyDomain set to your subdomain. Requests go to https://{companyDomain}.bamboohr.com/api/v1/.

notes: Responses default to XML; every tool sends Accept: application/json to get JSON
List Time-Off Requests requires both start and end dates (YYYY-MM-DD)
Whether you can create or approve time-off requests depends on the permissions of the user who owns the API key
